You glance out the window and nod, taking in the frozen ball of the moon below, hanging in the shadow of the massive red gas giant in the background. There’s a frigid beauty to the world, that much is totally undeniable... but it’s a lot more comfortable to enjoy from the heated heights of the space station.

The girls have followed your gaze, hand in hand. Farine’s kitty-tail flicks from side to side as she takes in the view, while Shierra’s draconic one remains curled tight around her chair. When your food finally arrives it’s almost an imposition, but the succulent smells inevitably draw your attentions back to the table, and to each other.
“So, you said your ship’s going to be in dock for a while?” you say, before cramming the first forkful of your meal in your mouth.

“Yeah,” her wife adds. “That’s why we put up the ExtraMeet thing. We wanted to make some friends while we’re stuck in dock.”
You give them a grin. “Your profile mentioned more than just friends.”
